step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("histopathology features of Goodpasture syndrome")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Thought: After reviewing the evidence, I note several key points. First, while option C — "linear IgG staining on immunofluorescence" — is indeed the hallmark finding in Goodpasture syndrome and directly correlates with anti-GBM antibody deposits, recent literature emphasizes that this linear deposition is highly specific to anti-GBM disease. Second, although many sources describe crescentic glomerulonephritis as a feature, and even mention necrotizing glomerulonephritis as a consequence of anti-GBM disease, none state that either of these features is *specific* to Goodpasture syndrome. For instance, crescentic glomerulonephritis appears broadly in various causes of RPGN (including post-infection, lupus nephritis), so it lacks specificity. Similarly, necrotizing vasculitis (option A) is seen in conditions such as vasculitis or immune-complex diseases—not exclusive to anti-GBM disease. Furthermore, option D ("longitudinal splitting and thickening of the GBM"), though described indirectly in some texts, isn't formally referenced as a specific histopathological marker in any source. Meanwhile, option B—"positive cryoglobulins"—is unrelated to anti-GBM disease; cryoglobulinemia refers to cold-agglutinating proteins and is more classically tied to monoclonal gammopathy or viral infections rather than Goodpasture syndrome.
Therefore, compared to all alternatives, linear IgG staining remains unmatched in specificity to anti-GBM disease. Even though the exact morphology may vary slightly between individuals, the linear IgG pattern on immunofluorescence continues to serve as the gold-standard diagnostic tool.
Final decision: I maintain agreement with the original conclusion.
Answer: \boxed{C}
---
Please ensure every sentence strictly adheres to instruction rules above. No extra commentary allowed. Each response must contain at most two sentences: brief agreement/disagreement with reasoning + clear boxed letter.
After reviewing updated evidence, I agree that linear IgG staining on immunofluorescence is the most specific histopathological feature of anti-GBM disease, as it defines the deposited autoantibodies targeting the glomerular basement membrane. All alternative choices lack specificity: crescentic/necrotizing inflammation overlaps with other RPGNs, cryoglobulins relate to different conditions, and longitudinal GBM changes are not officially recognized markers.
